steps.title: Identify the causal framework
steps.content: The notes describe a causal chain: reduced hunting time (from abundant tuna) leads to understimulation, which increases interactions with vessels. The researchers explicitly state this connection: “this shift has increased the number of interactions between marine vessels and understimulated orcas.” Since the documented interactions are destructive (ramming, nudging, biting), this causal chain explains why the destructive behavior occurs.

steps.title: Apply the framework to the choices
steps.content: A claims the destructive behavior stems from understimulation and supports this by invoking the researchers' causal chain: less hunting leading to increased vessel interactions. B reverses the causality in the notes by claiming orca behavior caused tuna increases, C falsely asserts TAST is effective despite the notes saying it's pending testing, and D simply reports facts without proposing an explanation. That matches choice A — A is correct.
